title: rosa pisocarpa subsp. pisocarpa
intro: Rosa pisocarpa subsp. pisocarpa, also known as the Western Pink Rose or Western Wild Rose, is a subspecies of fragrant shrub native to the northwestern region of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Rosa pisocarpa subsp. pisocarpa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and provide relief from digestive issues, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. The herbal preparations for this plant include tea, salve, and infused oil, which can be used topically or internally to promote overall well-being.
